11 March 2026 Military News

News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

  • War Department Continues to Encourage Civilians to Augment Homeland Security Border Mission DoD News 11 Mar 2026 -- Last year, the War Department announced a unique opportunity for department civilians to serve at the Department of Homeland Security and support DHS as it works to keep America safe.

  • CGC Forward interdicts suspected drug smugglers in Eastern Pacific Sea US Coast Guard 11 Mar 2026 -- Coast Guard Cutter Forward (WMEC 911) delivered a decisive blow to maritime criminal networks by intercepting a self-propelled semi-submersible (SPSS) vessel during a routine patrol on February 24 in the Eastern Pacific Ocean.

    • Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • Civilians Warned to Avoid Ports Used by Iranian Forces CENTCOM 11 Mar 2026 -- On March 11, U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) is issuing a warning to civilians that the Iranian regime is using civilian ports along the Strait of Hormuz to conduct military operations that threaten international shipping.
  • US burns through two years' worth of Patriot missile production in war on Iran: Report Press TV 11 Mar 2026 -- The US military is facing a "historic challenge" as it attempts to counter Iran's vast arsenal of "low-cost" drones and ballistic missiles, says a new report.
  • US troops killed; at least 17 sites damaged in Iran's reprisal attacks: Report Press TV 11 Mar 2026 -- A recent analysis has revealed that Iran's retaliatory missile and drone strikes on American military installations and other assets across West Asia have damaged a number of sites and killed US troops.
  • US Navy denies oil tanker escorts after energy secretary's lie sparks confusion Press TV 11 Mar 2026 -- The US Navy has denied near-daily requests from the shipping industry for military escorts through the Strait of Hormuz since the onset of the US-Israeli aggression on Iran.
  • NY Times: Trump miscalculated Iran's retaliation as war costs soar Press TV 11 Mar 2026 -- US President Donald Trump miscalculated Iran's response to the ongoing aggression by a US-Israeli coalition, while the Strait of Hormuz remains virtually impassable with oil prices hiking up, The New York Times says.
  • US Forces Destroyed 16 Iranian Minelayers Near Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday - CENTCOM Sputnik 11 Mar 2026 -- The US forces destroyed on Tuesday 16 Iranian minelayers near the Strait of Hormuz, the US Central Command (CENTCOM) said.

  • Commercial Ships Targeted As Iran Threatens Key Gulf Energy Transit Point RFE/RL 11 Mar 2026 -- Three vessels were damaged in incidents across the Persian Gulf's key maritime artery, where about a fifth of the world's oil and gas supplies travel, as Iran continued to launch air attacks around the Middle East.
  • IRGC launches 40th wave of Operation True Promise 4 in cooperation with Hezbollah Press TV 11 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) says it has carried out the 40th wave of its ongoing retaliatory Operation True Promise 4, launching decisive strikes against hostile targets throughout the region.
  • We'll open gates of fire upon Israel, US; enemies won't be safe even at home: IRGC Quds Force Press TV 11 Mar 2026 -- The Quds Force of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has issued a forceful statement, denouncing the United States and the Israeli regime for their ongoing unprovoked aggression against Iran and promising unwavering retaliation.
  • True Promise 4: Iran and resistance axis ops. against US-Israeli assets on Mar. 11 Press TV 11 Mar 2026 -- Press TV Website Staff
  • 2nd cargo vessel in hours hit by 'unknown projectile' in Strait of Hormuz: UK Maritime Trade Operations Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 11 Mar 2026 -- A cargo vessel was struck by "an unknown projectile" in the Strait of Hormuz early Wednesday, marking the second such incident reported within hours in one of the world's most critical shipping lanes, Anadolu Agency reported, citing the UK Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO).
  • 'All ports in Persian Gulf will be legitimate targets if Iranian ports are attacked' Press TV 11 Mar 2026 -- The senior spokesman of Iran's armed forces has warned that all ports and economic centers in Persian Gulf littoral states will be considered legitimate targets for Iran if the United States attacks Iranian ports as part of the ongoing joint aggression with Israel against the country.
  • Any ship passing Strait of Hormuz must obtain approval: IRGC Navy chief Press TV 11 Mar 2026 -- The commander of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) Naval Force says any vessel seeking to sail through the Strait of Hormuz must obtain Iran's approval; otherwise, it could become a target of Iranian attacks.
  • 100 American soldiers wounded in Iran's attack on Kuwait's al-Udairi base: IRGC Press TV 11 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has announced that around 100 American "terrorists" have been injured in a retaliatory attack by Iran's navy on al-Udairi base in Kuwait.
  • IRGC strikes 2 vessels defying warnings in Strait of Hormuz Press TV 11 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) Navy has conducted strikes on two commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz after they repeatedly ignored warnings.
  • In Numbers: Civilian toll rises as US-Israeli aggression against Iran enters 12th day Press TV 11 Mar 2026

  • Is The US Considering Seizing Iran's Kharg Island? RFE/RL 11 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Kharg Island, the country's main oil export outlet, could be a target for the United States, experts say, in a move that could cut off the revenues that sustain Tehran's sanctions-hit economy.
  • Ranking Member Shaheen Joins Over 40 Senators in Pressing DOD for Answers on School Bombing and Civilian Casualties in Iran US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 11 Mar 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, Chris Van Hollen (D-MD), Tim Kaine (D-VA), Elizabeth Warren (D-MA) and Brian Schatz (D-HI) were joined by Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (D-NY) and 40 colleagues in pressing the Department of Defense to provide answers to the American people on the airstrikes on a school in Minab, Iran and civilian casualties in the Trump Administration's war on Iran, as well as accountability for those responsible.
  • Statement from Assistant Secretary Dylan Johnson on Successful Operations to Assist American Citizens in the Middle East US Dept. of State 11 Mar 2026

  • Iranian Officials Seek To Quell Rumors About Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei's Health RFE/RL 11 Mar 2026 -- Iranian officials went on the offensive on March 11 to knock down rumors over newly appointed Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei's state of health, saying he was injured in the air strikes that killed his father and other family members but is "safe and sound."
  • Iran slams EU over new 'immoral and unlawful' sanctions amid US-Israeli aggression Press TV 11 Mar 2026 -- Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Baghaei vehemently condemned the European Union's decision to impose new sanctions on 19 Iranian officials and entities.
  • Iran says enemy should await 'painful retaliation' after hitting bank Press TV 11 Mar 2026 -- An Iranian military official says following the US-Israeli attack to one of the country's banks, the enemies should await Iran's painful retaliation.
  • Iran urges global condemnation of US-Israeli aggression, gets strong support Press TV 11 Mar 2026 -- Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian has urged international bodies to denounce the US-Israeli aggression against Iran's civilian infrastructure and ordinary people, stressing the need to hold them accountable for their crimes.
  • 'Heinous crime': Iran denounces Israeli assassination of 4 Iranian diplomats in Lebanon Press TV 11 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Ambassador to the United Nations, Amir Saeid Iravani says the Israeli regime has assassinated four senior Iranian diplomats in a "heinous crime" in the Lebanese capital Beirut.

  • Security Council Adopts Resolution 2817 (2026) Condemning Iran's 'Egregious Attacks' Against Neighbours as Middle East Violence Rapidly Spirals UN Security Council 11 Mar 2026 -- The Security Council today adopted a resolution condemning Iran's "egregious attacks" against its regional neighbours amid rapidly spiraling violence in the Middle East, while rejecting a second draft tabled by the Russian Federation.
  • Security Council adopts Gulf-backed resolution condemning Iran attacks; Russia bid fails UN News 11 Mar 2026 -- As war continues in the Middle East, the UN Security Council on Wednesday adopted a Bahrain-led resolution condemning Iranian attacks on several regional states, while a separate draft resolution introduced by Russia on the wider crisis failed to pass.
  • Iranian civilians caught between ongoing armed hostilities and repression that has reached unprecedented levels, which may amount to crimes against humanity, UN Fact-Finding Mission says OHCHR 11 Mar 2026 -- The Independent International Fact-Finding Mission on the Islamic Republic of Iran warned today that the country's deepening human rights crisis - already characterized by institutionalized repression on an unprecedented scale, including violations that may amount to crimes against humanity - is likely to worsen in the wake of the US-Israeli attacks on Iran and Iran's retaliatory strikes across the region.
  • UN warns of higher food prices, cost-of-living if Strait of Hormuz shuts Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 11 Mar 2026 -- United Nations: The United Nations has warned of significant risks to global trade and development, including higher food prices and cost-of-living, if the Strait of Hormuz is closed amid the West Asia conflict, according to The Economic Times.
  • Strait of Hormuz disruptions: Implications for global trade and development Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 11 Mar 2026 -- The Strait of Hormuz is one of the world's most critical maritime chokepoints, carrying around a quarter of global seaborne oil trade and significant volumes of liquefied natural gas and fertilizers, according to the official website of the UNCTAD.
  • UN relief chief condemns '$1 billion-a-day' cost of war in Middle East UN News 11 Mar 2026 -- The UN's emergency relief chief on Wednesday condemned the "$1 billion-a-day" cost of the war in the Middle East, at a time when humanitarian needs are soaring and aid funding is falling dangerously short.
  • Child casualties rise amidst deepening Middle East conflict UNICEF 11 Mar 2026
  • Conflict deepens health crisis across Middle East, WHO says WHO 11 Mar 2026

  • IEA Member countries to carry out largest ever oil stock release amid market disruptions from Middle East conflict International Energy Agency (IEA) 11 Mar 2026 -- The 32 Member countries of the International Energy Agency unanimously agreed today to make 400 million barrels of oil from their emergency reserves available to the market to address disruptions in oil markets stemming from the war in the Middle East.
  • IEA proposes largest ever oil release from strategic reserves — WSJ Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 11 Mar 2026 -- The International Energy Agency has proposed the largest release of oil reserves in its history to bring down the crude prices that soared triggered by the US-Israel war with Iran, The Wall Street Journal reported citing officials familiar with the matter.

  • Our goal is to promote an immediate ceasefire, push all parties back on the track of dialogue, negotiations: FM says on focus of China's shuttle diplomacy regarding Iran Global Times 11 Mar 2026 -- In response to a question on whether the shuttle diplomacy carried out by special envoy of the Chinese government on the Middle East issue and Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi focuses solely on the Gulf region or also extends to Israel and the US in order to push for a ceasefire, or at least a ceasefire for now, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Wednesday that, "I just introduced relevant efforts China has made on mediation. You may have also noticed that Foreign Minister Wang Yi recently held a phone conversation with Israeli Foreign Minister, and the special envoy of the Chinese government on the Middle East issue is currently conducting shuttle visits in the region."
  • China will never stop striving for peace, and its voice in safeguarding fairness, justice will never be late: FM on China's effort in de-escalating tensions Global Times 11 Mar 2026 -- The current fighting is a war that should never have happened and does no one any good, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said at a regular press conference on Wednesday, citing remarks of Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi, when asked to further elaborate on China's position on the Iran situation and its efforts to help de-escalate tensions and how to view the next development of the situation.
  • Continuation and Escalation of Hostilities Produces No Winner; Military Operations Must Be Immediately Stopped Global Times 11 Mar 2026
  • Relevant parties should immediately stop military operations, jointly uphold peace and stability in Middle East: Defense Spokesperson PRC MND 11 Mar 2026 -- "The continuation and escalation of hostilities produces no winner, and will only push the Middle East into a dangerous abyss, inflicting greater suffering on the peoples of the region," said Senior Colonel Jiang Bin, spokesperson for China's Ministry of National Defense, at a regular press briefing on Wednesday.

  • Telephone conversation with President of the UAE Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan President of Russia 11 Mar 2026 -- Vladimir Putin had a telephone conversation with President of the UAE Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan.
  • Putin Holds Telephone Conversation With President of UAE Sputnik 11 Mar 2026 -- Russian President Vladimir Putin held a telephone conversation with UAE President Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, the Kremlin said on Wednesday.
  • Telephone conversation with President of Azerbaijan Ilham Aliyev President of Russia 11 Mar 2026
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's telephone conversation with Foreign Minister of Oman Badr bin Hamad Al Busaidi Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 11 Mar 2026
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's telephone conversation with Minister of Foreign Affairs, International Cooperation and Egyptian Expatriates of the Arab Republic of Egypt Badr Abdelatty Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 11 Mar 2026

  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Three Ballistic Missiles Launched Toward Prince Sultan Air Base Intercepted and Destroyed SPA 11 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: A Drone Intercepted and Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 11 Mar 2026
  • HRH the Crown Prince, Indonesian President Discuss Regional Military Escalation by Phone SPA 11 Mar 2026 -- His Royal Highness Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Crown Prince and Prime Minister, received a phone call today from President Prabowo Subianto of the Republic of Indonesia
  • HRH the Crown Prince, Iraqi Prime Minister Discuss Regional Military Escalation SPA 11 Mar 2026 -- His Royal Highness Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Crown Prince and Prime Minister, received a phone call today from Prime Minister of the Republic of Iraq Mohammed Shia' al-Sudani , during which they discussed the military escalation in the region and its repercussions on security and stability
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: A Drone Heading Toward Shaybah Field Intercepted and Destroyed in the Empty Quarter SPA 11 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Four Drones Intercepted, Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 11 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Two Drones Intercepted, Destroyed in Empty Quarter Heading toward Shaybah Field SPA 11 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Two Drones Intercepted, Destroyed in Empty Quarter Heading toward Shaybah Field SPA 11 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Five Drones Intercepted, Destroyed East of Al-Kharj Governorate SPA 11 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Two Drones Intercepted, Destroyed in Hafr Al-Batin SPA 11 Mar 202
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Six Ballistic Missiles Launched toward Prince Sultan Air Base Intercepted, Destroyed SPA 11 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Ballistic Missile Launched toward Eastern Region Intercepted, Destroyed SPA 11 Mar 2026

  • G7 ready to take 'all necessary measures' with IEA to safeguard energy supplies Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 11 Mar 2026 -- Energy ministers from the Group of Seven (G7) said they are ready to take "all necessary measures" in coordination with the International Energy Agency (IEA) to address risks to global energy supplies stemming from the conflict in the Middle East, Anadolu Agency reports citing to a statement released on Wednesday.
  • PM call with G7 leaders: 11 March 2026 Gov.UK 11 Mar 2026 -- The Prime Minister spoke to G7 leaders this afternoon to discuss the latest situation in the Middle East.
  • Prime Minister Carney participates in a virtual G7 Leaders' meeting on the situation in the Middle East Prime Minister of Canada 11 Mar 2026 -- Today, the Prime Minister, Mark Carney, participated in a virtual G7 Leaders' meeting on the situation in the Middle East.
  • Speech by President von der Leyen at the European Parliament plenary debate in preparation of the European Council meeting of 19-20 March 2026 and on the US-Israel military operations against the Iranian regime, its consequences and the need to support the people of Iran European Commission 11 Mar 2026
  • HE GCCSG Condemns in the Strongest Terms the Brutal Iranian Targeting of Fuel Storage Tanks at Salalah Port in the Sultanate of Oman Gulf Cooperation Council 11 Mar 2026
  • Ayatollah Sistani prays Iran will triumph over 'ill-wishers' as he congratulates new Leader Press TV 11 Mar 2026 -- Grand Ayatollah Sayyid Ali al-Sistani, the revered Shia religious authority based in Najaf, has extended his congratulations to Ayatollah Seyyed Mojtaba Khamenei on his election as the new Leader of the Islamic Revolution of Iran, expressing hope for his success in serving the great Iranian nation and repelling the "ill-wishers".
  • Hezbollah, Islamic Jihad vow support for new Leader of Islamic Revolution Press TV 11 Mar 2026 -- Hezbollah and Palestinian Islamic Jihad leaders have congratulated Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei on his election as Leader of the Islamic Revolution, wishing him success in advancing the aspirations of the oppressed people around the world.
  • Answer Given by Spokesperson for DPRK Foreign Ministry KCNA 11 Mar 2026 -- A spokesperson for the Foreign Ministry of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea gave the following answer to a question raised by a journalist on March 10 as regards the fact that the U.S. and its vassal forces are getting undisguised in their intervention into the internal affairs of Iran over the recent election of its new leader of the Islamic Revolution:
  • The draft Russian resolution does nothing to address the concerns of those in the region who have faced unprovoked and unjustifiable attacks: UK Explanation of Vote at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 11 Mar 2026 -- Explanation of Vote by Ambassador James Kariuki, UK Chargé d'Affaires to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on the Middle East.
  • The adoption of this resolution sends a clear message from the Council condemning Iran's reckless attacks against the Gulf States and Jordan: UK Explanation of Vote at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 11 Mar 2026 -- Explanation of Vote by Ambassador James Kariuki, UK Chargé d'Affaires to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on the Middle East.
  • President of the Republic of Cyprus: Cyprus is always part of the solution, never part of the problem Republic of Cyprus 11 Mar 2026
  • "We continue our efforts to facilitate a return to negotiations" Presidency of The Republic of Turkey 11 Mar 2026 -- Addressing AK Party's parliamentary group meeting, President Erdoğan said: "This war must be stopped before it escalates and completely engulfs the region in fire. If diplomacy is given a chance, this is surely possible. We are patiently continuing our efforts to facilitate a return to the table and to negotiations, and a re-engagement in diplomacy."

  • Interview: Amid Ongoing Public Absence, Is Iran's New Leader In Control? RFE/RL 11 Mar 2026 -- Iran's new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, is probably in charge despite making no public appearance since being appointed on March 8 and amid reports that he was injured in an air strike, according to a leading Israeli expert on Iran.
  • Oil, War, And The Strait of Hormuz: Can Washington Safeguard Global Energy Markets From Iran? RFE/RL 11 Mar 2026 -- As the United States and Israel's conflict with Iran converges on the Strait of Hormuz -- the world's most critical energy chokepoint -- the economic consequences of a major disruption to oil and gas supplies could reverberate around the world.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Hegseth Calls for Assessment, Alignment of DOW Legal Functions, Operations DoD News 11 Mar 2026 -- Secretary of War Pete Hegseth today called on the joint forces service secretaries to work through their general counsels to conduct a "ruthless, no-excuses review" of the War Department's assorted legal support functions and operations.
  • U.S. Coast Guard Cutter Polar Star completes Operation Deep Freeze 2026 mission, departs Antarctica US Coast Guard 11 Mar 2026 -- The U.S. Coast Guard Cutter Polar Star (WAGB 10) departed McMurdo Sound, Antarctica, on March 1, after operating for 55 days below the Antarctic Circle and traveling 14,000 miles in support of Operation Deep Freeze 2026.
  • USS Mustin to forward deploy to Japan US Navy 11 Mar 2026 -- Arleigh Burke-class guided-missile destroyer USS Mustin (DDG 89) will forward deploy to Yokosuka, Japan, as part of a scheduled rotation of forces in the Pacific. This move will be a permanent change of station for the crew and family members.
  • Joint Statement on the U.S.-France Deterrence, Strategic Stability, and Non-Proliferation Dialogue US Dept. of State 11 Mar 2026 -- The text of the following statement was released by the Governments of the United States of America and France on the occasion of the U.S.-France Deterrence, Strategic Stability, and Non-proliferation Dialogue.

  • Swedish Air Force concludes first Icelandic Air Policing Mission NATO Allied Air Command 11 Mar 2026 -- The Swedish Air Force has successfully concluded its deployment to Keflavík Air Base, Iceland, where JAS 39 Gripen fighter aircraft conducted NATO's Icelandic Air Policing mission as part of the Alliance's Arctic Sentry activities.
  • Secretary General praises the close cooperation between NATO and Moldova NATO 11 Mar 2026 -- On Wednesday (11 March 2026), NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte welcomed the Deputy Prime Minister of the Republic of Moldova Mihai Popșoi to NATO Headquarters, where he participated in a meeting of the North Atlantic Council.
  • NATO Deputy Secretary General welcomes President of the ICRC to NATO Headquarters NATO 11 Mar 2026 -- On Wednesday (11 March 2026), NATO Deputy Secretary General Radmila Shekerinska welcomed Mirjana Spoljaric Egger, President of the International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC), to the North Atlantic Council.
  • NATO Secretary General praises European contribution to transatlantic security NATO 11 Mar 2026 -- On Monday 9 March, NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte took part in a panel discussion in Brussels, together with EU leaders, as part of the annual EU Ambassadors' Conference.

  • The Ministry of Defence contracted a record number of multirotor drones for the front Ukraine MOD 11 Mar 2026
  • Combat brigades received 500,000 items, including drones, ground robotic systems, and other equipment, through DOT-Chain Defence Ukraine MOD 11 Mar 2026
  • Ukrainian rescue service leaders bring lessons in resilience to NATO NATO 11 Mar 2026 -- On Tuesday and Wednesday (10-11 March 2026), senior officials from the State Emergency Service of Ukraine (SESU) visited NATO HQ to brief Allies on crisis management, resilience-building, and civil-military cooperation to keep citizens safe.

  • Regular Press Briefing of the Ministry of National Defense on March 11, 2026 PRC MND 11 Mar 2026
  • Human Primacy Must Be Upheld in Military Applications of AI and to Avoid Technological Runaway Global Times 11 Mar 2026
  • Human primacy must be upheld in military applications of AI: Defense Spokesperson PRC MND 11 Mar 2026

  • West Coast training prepares NZ Army soldiers for upcoming Indo-Pacific exercises New Zealand Defence Force 11 Mar 2026 -- Soldiers from the New Zealand Army's 2nd/1st Battalion, Royal New Zealand Infantry Regiment (RNZIR) have honed their jungle, or close-country, skills on the South Island's West Coast.
  • Defence, Navy and industry complete sovereign radar tracking trial off Banks Peninsula New Zealand Defence Force 11 Mar 2026 -- The Royal New Zealand Navy, Defence Science and Technology (DST), and Dawn Aerospace, have completed a live radar tracking experiment using a reusable rocket-powered aircraft off the Canterbury coast.

  • Destroyer Choe Hyon Conducts Test-Fire of Strategic Cruise Missiles KCNA 11 Mar 2026 -- Amid the continued tests of operational efficiency of the Destroyer Choe Hyon of the Navy of the Korean People's Army, a strategic cruise missile test-fire was conducted again on March 10.

  • UK convenes JEF partners to disrupt Russia's shadow fleet UK MOD 11 Mar 2026 -- Defence Secretary and Attorney General met JEF legal representatives to address action against Russia's sanction-evading shadow fleet funding Ukraine war.

  • Defence Minister McGuinty met with Japan's Minister of Defense Canada 11 Mar 2026 -- Over the weekend, the Honourable David J. McGuinty, Minister of National Defence, met with Japan's Minister of Defense, Shinjiro Koizumi, during his visit to Tokyo, with Prime Minister Mark Carney.

  • India is Committed to welfare of its Neighbours: Raksha Rajya Mantri India PIB 11 Mar 2026 -- Raksha Rajya Mantri Shri Sanjay Seth said today that India is committed to welfare of its neighbours, while steadfast on zero-tolerance towards terrorism
  • INDIAN NAVAL SHIP TRIKAND AT PORT LOUIS, MAURITIUS India PIB 11 Mar 2026 -- Indian Naval Ship Trikand, stealth frigate of the Indian Navy, called at Port Louis, Mauritius on 10 Mar 2026 during her ongoing operational deployment to the South West Indian Ocean Region.

  • Retreating to militarism leads nowhere but self-destruction: Chinese MND on Japan's move to deploy long-range missiles Global Times 11 Mar 2026 -- When asked to comment on reports that Japan has recently started to field long-range missiles with a range of approximately 1,000 kilometers, capable of reaching coastal areas of neighboring countries, Jiang Bin, a spokesperson for China's Ministry of National Defense, said at a regular press conference on Wednesday that with the planned fielding of long-range offensive weapons whose range far exceeds its territorial boundaries, Japan has completely stripped away its pretense of "exclusively defense-oriented" principle, "passive defense" strategy and "self-defense" policy.
  • Message to the Japanese Side: Retreating to its Belligerent and Militarist Past Leads Nowhere but Self-destruction Global Times 11 Mar 2026
  • Old path of belligerent militarism leads nowhere but self-destruction: Defense Spokesperson PRC MND 11 Mar 2026 -- "Should Japan dare to violate China's sovereignty and security by force, it will inevitably meet a head-on blow and suffer a greater defeat," said Senior Colonel Jiang Bin, spokesperson for China's Ministry of National Defense, at a press briefing on Wednesday.

  • U.S. forces in S. Korea preparing to relocate THAAD interceptors to Middle East: media People's Daily 11 Mar 2026 -- U.S. forces in South Korea were preparing to relocate interceptor missiles of the Terminal High Altitude Area Defense (THAAD) battery to the Middle East, a local newspaper said Wednesday.
  • China's position of opposing deployment of THAAD anti-missile system in ROK remains unchanged, FM says on US' relocation of system to Middle East Global Times 11 Mar 2026 -- When asked to comment on reports that the US Department of Defense has moved parts of Terminal High Altitude Area Defense (THAAD) air defense system from South Korea to the Middle East, and if China opposes the US redeploying these weapons back to South Korea in the future, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Wednesday that "we've noted relevant reports. China's position of opposing the deployment of the US THAAD anti-missile system in South Korea remains unchanged."

  • Turkish Land Forces Commander visits Combined Arms Army Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 11 Mar 2026 -- On March 11, a delegation led by the Commander of the Land Forces of the Armed Forces of the Republic of Türkiye, Army General Metin Tokel visited the Nakhchivan Autonomous Republic, the Ministry of Defense told AZERTAC.

  • Statement to the 111th Session of the Executive Council of the OPCW UK FCDO 11 Mar 2026 -- Statement by His Excellency Chris Rampling, Permanent Representative of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland at the 111th Executive Council of the Organisation for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons.

  • A look at the Defence news 2 - 8 March Netherlands Ministry of Defence 11 Mar 2026

  • Norwegian F-35s Identify Russian Aircraft Forsvaret - Norwegian Armed Forces 11 Mar 2026 -- Norwegian fighter jets have scrambled for two consecutive days to identify Russian military aircraft in international airspace off Finnmark.

  • Chiefs of General Staff of Azerbaijani and Serbian Armies hold phone talk Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 11 Mar 2026 -- On March 11, Azerbaijan's First Deputy Minister of Defense, Chief of the General Staff of the Azerbaijan Army Colonel General Karim Valiyev held a phone conversation with Chief of the General Staff of the Armed Forces of the Republic of Serbia General Milan Mojsilović. The conversation was held at the initiative of the Serbian side, the Ministry of Defense told AZERTAC.

  • PH, US seek enhanced defense ties, 'stable, secure' Indo-Pacific Philippine News Agency 11 Mar 2026 -- The Philippines and the United States (US) have agreed to strengthen their defense cooperation and collaborate to ensure a "stable and secure" Indo-Pacific region.

  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Ethiopian Prime Minister Abiy US Dept. of State 11 Mar 2026

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 11 March 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 11 Mar 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Hezbollah takes Israeli targets under massive retaliatory rocket salvo Press TV 11 Mar 2026 -- Hezbollah has announced taking multiple Israeli targets under a massive rocket salvo in response to Tel Aviv's violations against Lebanese sovereignty.

  • UN Relief Chief tells Security Council: An "exhausted" Lebanon "is not asking for help, but for oxygen" UNOCHA 11 Mar 2026 -- Briefing to the Security Council on the humanitarian situation in Lebanon by Tom Fletcher, Under-Secretary-General for Humanitarian Affairs and Emergency Relief Coordinator
  • Lebanon 'Exhausted by Other People's Wars', Security Council Hears from Humanitarian Affairs Chief amid Rising Civilian Toll UN Security Council 11 Mar 2026 -- "Lebanon is exhausted by other people's wars", the Security Council heard today during a briefing on the country, as Iran and Israel were urged to stop fighting their war in its territory.

  • Israel/Palestine: Statement by the Spokesperson on the situation in the West Bank European External Action Service (EEAS) 11 Mar 2026
  • Foreign Ministers of Saudi Arabia and Several Arab and Islamic Countries Condemn the Continued Closure of Al-Aqsa Mosque/Haram Al-Sharif by Israeli Occupation Authorities SPA 11 Mar 2026 -- The Foreign Ministers of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan, the United Arab Emirates, the State of Qatar, the Republic of Indonesia, the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, the Arab Republic of Egypt, and the Republic of Türkiye condemn Israeli occupation authorities' continued closure of the gates of Al-Aqsa Mosque / Al-Haram Al-Sharif to Muslim worshippers particularly during the holy month of Ramadan
  • Joint Statement by the Foreign Ministers of Türkiye, Egypt, Indonesia, Jordan, Pakistan, Qatar, Saudi Arabia and UAE, 11 March 2026 Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 11 Mar 2026
  • The United Kingdom is working closely with the Lebanese Government and humanitarian partners to support those affected by the current escalation: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 11 Mar 2026 -- Statement by Ambassador James Kariuki, UK Chargé d'Affaires to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on Lebanon.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: 87 million lives campaign, Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Ukraine UNOCHA 11 Mar 2026

  • UN Relief Chief: Choose humanity to save 87 million lives in a time of "brutality" UNOCHA 11 Mar 2026 -- Remarks at Press Conference on 87 Million Lives Campaign by Tom Fletcher, UN Under-Secretary-General for Humanitarian Affairs and Emergency Relief Coordinator

  • Secretary's Response to Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi's Statement on the South China Sea Philippines DFA 11 Mar 2026 -- The Philippines notes the recent remarks of Foreign Minister Wang Yi regarding the situation in the South China Sea. As ASEAN Chair in 2026, the Philippines approaches this issue with a clear sense of responsibility to the region and to the international community.
  • PH vows to push for SCS peace, 'legally binding' ASEAN COC Philippine News Agency 11 Mar 2026 -- The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) on Wednesday said the Philippines, as the 2026 chair of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN), will work constructively with China and fellow member states to advance a "legally binding" Code of Conduct (COC) in the South China Sea.
  • Resupply missions in WPS to continue despite spying Philippine News Agency 11 Mar 2026 -- The military's rotation and resupply (RORE) missions for Filipino troops manning the various outposts in the West Philippine Sea (WPS) will continue despite these operations being specifically targeted by the neutralized espionage ring, a ranking naval official said on Wednesday.

  • ICC Appeals Chamber confirms decision on inadmissibility of the case against Edmond Beina ICC 11 Mar 2026 -- Today, 11 March 2026, the Appeals Chamber of the International Criminal Court (ICC) confirmed Pre Trial Chamber II's Decision on the Central African Republic's challenge to the admissibility of the case against Edmond Beina.

  • PH security landscape 'stabilizing'; 413 Reds, supporters neutralized Philippine News Agency 11 Mar 2026 -- Around 413 New People's Army (NPA) rebels and their supporters were reported "neutralized" by government troops from Jan. 1 to March 5, the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) said late Tuesday afternoon.

  • Armenian PM: Armenia has no intention and no reason to delay the implementation of TRIPP project Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 11 Mar 2026 -- "For eight months now, there is complete peace on the Armenia-Azerbaijan border. The year 2025 became the first year since our independence in 1991, the first year when there were no casualties and injuries due to the Armenia-Azerbaijan shootings," said Nikol Pashinyan, Prime Minister of Armenia, as he addressed the European Parliament in Strasbourg.

  • UK Marks 30 Years of Dayton Article IV and Sub-Regional Arms Control: UK statement to the OSCE UK FCDO 11 Mar 2026 -- UK Senior Military Advisor, Lt Col Joby Rimmer, marks 30 years of Dayton Article IV, highlighting its role in arms control and regional stability, reaffirming support for Bosnia and Herzegovina, and urging modernisation to keep the Agreement effective amid evolving security challenges

News Reports

  • EU Statement at CND69 on International Narcotics Control Board, 11 March 2026 European External Action Service (EEAS) 11 Mar 2026

  • Comment by Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova on the attack on the civilian commercial vessel Arctic Metagaz Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 11 Mar 2026 -- At 4:25 a.m. local time on March 3, the Russian-flagged liquefied natural gas carrier Arctic Metagaz, which was carrying 100,000 cubic metres of LNG and a crew of 30 Russian sailors, was attacked by maritime and aerial drones in neutral waters in the central Mediterranean Sea, 168 nautical miles southeast of the coast of Malta.
  • Meeting with Education Minister Sergei Kravtsov President of Russia 11 Mar 2026 -- Vladimir Putin met with Minister of Education Sergei Kravtsov in the Kremlin. The discussion focused on drafting the Russian Education System Development Strategy, creating a unified set of state textbooks, improving the quality of education in natural sciences, and training teachers and specialists for colleges.
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's remarks at the first Ministerial Conference of the International Organisation for the Russian Language, Moscow, March 11, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 11 Mar 2026
  • Foreign Minister Tsahkna in Canberra: former Russian combatants may pose a threat to Australia as well Estonia MFA 11 Mar 2026 -- During meetings held in Canberra today, 11 March, Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna discussed the threat posed by Russian fighters, the need to establish a special tribunal to hold Russia accountable, and cooperation in continuing support for Ukraine.

  • China's top political advisory body holds closing meeting of annual session People's Daily 11 Mar 2026 -- The 14th National Committee of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference (CPPCC), China's top political advisory body, concluded its annual session on Wednesday morning.
  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on March 11, 2026 PRC MofA 11 Mar 2026
  • Military deputies discuss Xi's speech at annual legislative session People's Daily 11 Mar 2026 -- Members of the military delegation attending the ongoing annual session of the national legislature studied and discussed President Xi Jinping's speech delivered at a plenary meeting of the delegation, a military spokesperson said Tuesday.
  • China's top political advisory body holds closing meeting of annual session Global Times 11 Mar 2026 -- The fourth session of the 14th National Committee of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference, China's top political advisory body, started its closing meeting on Wednesday morning.
  • China schedules next moonshot this year: developer Global Times 11 Mar 2026 -- China's robust development in the domain of deep-space exploration will see new advancement, as the country schedules the launch of its Chang'e-7 lunar probe this year to target the Moon's south pole in search of water ice - a critical resource for future sustained human presence and base construction on the Moon, the Global Times learned from the state-owned space program contractor China Aerospace Science and Technology Corporation (CASC) on Wednesday.

  • Exclusive: PLA tracks, monitors US P-8A patrol aircraft transiting Taiwan Straits Global Times 11 Mar 2026
  • "Taiwan Independence" Separatist Activities and Political Manipulation Are Futile Global Times 11 Mar 2026
  • "Taiwan Independence" Separatist Activities Are Futile: Defense Spokesperson PRC MND 11 Mar 2026 -- "The so-called goodwill mission is merely an excuse for the DPP authorities to solicit foreign support for "Taiwan independence," said a Chinese defense spokesperson on Wednesday.
  • DPP separatist moves under cover of trade, investment doomed to fail: Chinese spokesperson People's Daily 11 Mar 2026 -- A Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson on Tuesday said the Taiwan Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) authorities have repeatedly used pretexts such as trade, the economy and investment to carry out "Taiwan independence" separatist activities on the international stage, adding that such attempts are doomed to fail.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 11 Mar 2026

  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: NUCLEAR ENERGY MISSION India PIB 11 Mar 2026 -- Under the Nuclear Energy Mission announced in the Union Budget 2025-26, a total budgetary provision of ₹20,000 crore has been made for the research, design, development, and deployment of Small Modular Reactors (SMRs).
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: NUCLEAR POWER GENERATION India PIB 11 Mar 2026 -- The conclusion of international cooperation agreements in 2008 enabled import of fuel for reactors under International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) Safeguards. The year- wise generation by reactors using imported fuel is given in Annexure.
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: DEVELOPMENT OF SMALL MODULAR REACTORS India PIB 11 Mar 2026 -- Under the Nuclear Energy Mission announced in the Union Budget 2025-26, a total budgetary provision of ₹20,000 crore has been made for the research, design, development, and deployment of Small Modular Reactors (SMRs)
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: ZERO CUSTOM DUTY ON IMPORTS FOR NUCLEAR SECTOR India PIB 11 Mar 2026 -- The Zero customs duty on the imports of goods required for nuclear power projects will result in reduction of the project cost and the unit cost of electricity generated
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: THORIUM-BASED POWER PROJECTS India PIB 11 Mar 2026 -- Deployment of thorium-based power at scale in the country in a manner so as to ensure a long term self-sustainable indigenous power production is one of the cornerstones of the Indian three stage nuclear power programme

  • Statement by the G7 Presidency. Élysée - President of France 11 Mar 2026 -- G7 Leaders held a videoconference on Wednesday, 11 March 2026, to discuss the economic consequences of the war in the Middle East. This was the first meeting of G7 Leaders since the beginning of the French Presidency of the G7 in 2026.
  • India - Jean-Noël Barrot's conversation with his Indian counterpart, Subrahmanyam Jaishankar (11 March 2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 11 Mar 2026

  • Solidarity and joint responsibility for Europe: Foreign Minister Wadephul visits Cyprus, Israel and Saudi Arabia Germany Federal Foreign Office 11 Mar 2026 -- Johann Wadephul met his Cypriot counterpart Constantinos Kombos and the Foreign Minister of the UAE, Sheikh Abdullah bin Zayed, in Cyprus. Their talks focused on the escalation in the Middle East, the Cypriot Presidency of the Council of the EU and joint responsibility for European security.

  • "Türkiye is a country whose democracy is stronger than ever" Presidency of The Republic of Turkey 11 Mar 2026 -- Speaking at an iftar dinner with former AK Party deputies, President Erdoğan said: "Even though some still persistently ignore, today, Türkiye, as a result of our collective efforts, is a richer, freer and more pluralistic country whose democracy is stronger than ever. Praise be to Allah, today, we are living in a country whose star is shining increasingly more brightly."

  • President Ilham Aliyev and President of European Council delivered press statements Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 11 Mar 2026 -- President of the Republic of Azerbaijan Ilham Aliyev and President of the European Council António Costa delivered press statements on March 11.
  • Release from the Press Service of the President Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 11 Mar 2026 -- On March 11, President of the Russian Federation Vladimir Putin made a phone call to President of the Republic of Azerbaijan Ilham Aliyev.
  • Joint press statement by President of the European Council, António Costa, and President of the Republic of Azerbaijan, Ilham Aliyev EC/CEU 11 Mar 2026
  • Press remarks by President António Costa following the meeting with President of Azerbaijan Ilham Aliyev EC/CEU 11 Mar 2026
